big_picture:
• Growth, inflation & the gaps in between: What latest RBI annual report says about where India stands — 6 sources

• Charges to be framed against Sukesh, Jacqueline — 3 sources
• West Asia crisis: Govt waives import duty on cotton till October 30 — 3 sources
• General Subramani takes charge as new Chief of Defence Staff — 3 sources

story_clusters.summary: The Delhi High Court quashed a GST bank account attachment after the petitioner furnished ₹1.5 crore security, while the Bombay High Court quashed a GST rectification order for lack of a personal hearing. Additionally, the Allahabad High Court quashed an FIR against an advocate in a ₹10 crore GST evasion case, and the Karnataka High Court quashed a GST appeal rejection citing an earlier ruling.

story_clusters.summary: The Supreme Court upheld a GST assessment after notices were sent to the taxpayer’s registered email and mobile number. The Court also ruled that the assignment of leasehold rights constitutes a transfer of immovable property and is not subject to GST. Additionally, the Court issued notices regarding the sealing of GST premises without denying access and stayed a High Court ruling that deemed portal uploads insufficient for communication.

story_clusters.summary: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) is planning to expand its digital rupee (CBDC) initiatives, including cross-border pilots and integration with welfare schemes, as part of ongoing digital currency experiments. Despite the growth in digital payments, a recent RBI survey indicates a strong preference for cash among users. The RBI continues to explore currency-related facilities and policy measures to support these efforts.

articles.summary: The Kerala High Court has reaffirmed that armed forces personnel invalided out of service due to schizophrenia are entitled to the benefit of statutory presumptions under military pension rules unless the authorities provide reasons to deny disability pension. articles.title: ITAT Quashes Reassessment as Section 148 Notice Was Issued After Limitation Expiry
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that a reassessment for Assessment Year 2015-16 was invalid because the tax department issued a notice under Section 148 after the six-year limitation period had expired. The notice, issued on April 23, 2022, was time-barred as the deadline was March 31, 2022. Consequently, the reassessment proceedings and the resulting tax order were quashed. This ruling underscores strict adherence to procedural timelines in tax administration.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that for AY 2015-16, the six-year limitation period under the unamended law expired on 31.03.2022. Since the notice under Section 148 was issued on 23.04.2022, it was time-barred. The reassessment proceedings and consequential assessment order were therefore quashed. The post ITAT Quashes Reassessment as Section 148 Notice Was Issued After Limitation Expiry appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: No Error in Anjuman Ishaat-e-Taleem Trust Verdict: SC Upholds TET Requirement for In-Service Teachers; Extends Compliance Deadline to 31 August 2028
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld a requirement for in-service teachers to qualify the Teacher Eligibility Test (TET) under the Right to Education (RTE) Act, stating that children's educational future cannot be compromised. The court extended the compliance deadline for this rule to August 31, 2028. The ruling pertains to the Anjuman Ishaat-e-Taleem Trust case and reinforces the child-centric nature of the RTE Act. The decision impacts teacher qualifications and compliance timelines across India.
articles.raw_summary: The RTE Act is a child centric legislation and must be read so. Service of teachers cannot come at the cost of educational future of the children. articles.title: ITAT Upholds Section 154 Rectification as TOLA Extended Limitation for Rectification Proceedings
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) upheld a rectification order under Section 154 of the Income Tax Act, ruling that CBDT notifications issued under the Taxation and Other Laws (Relaxation and Amendment of Certain Provisions) Act (TOLA) extended the limitation period for departmental rectification actions. This decision saved the order from being time-barred. The ruling clarifies the interplay between statutory timelines and government notifications in tax proceedings.

articles.title: Rajasthan HC Upholds GST on BOT Highway Project as Toll Rights Constitute Consideration for Construction Services
articles.summary: The Rajasthan High Court upheld the Goods and Services Tax (GST) liability on Build-Operate-Transfer (BOT) highway projects, ruling that toll collection rights granted under concession agreements qualify as consideration for construction services. The court's decision affirms that such arrangements are subject to GST, impacting infrastructure financing and tax compliance. The ruling applies to road construction projects under BOT models in Rajasthan. This judgment clarifies the tax treatment of

articles.title: SC Examines Constitutional Validity of Section 16(2)(c) as Bona Fide Recipient Denied ITC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court is reviewing whether Section 16(2)(c) of the Central Goods and Services Tax (CGST) Act is constitutionally valid. The provision denies input tax credit (ITC) to a recipient even if the tax was paid by their immediate supplier. The case questions whether a bona fide recipient should be penalized for alleged defaults by a supplier further up the supply chain.
articles.raw_summary: The Supreme Court took up a challenge to Section 16(2)(c) of the CGST Act where ITC was denied despite tax having been paid by the immediate supplier. The case raises the issue of whether a recipient can be penalized for alleged default by a supplier further up the supply chain. The post SC Examines Constitutional Validity of Section 16(2)(c) as Bona Fide Recipient Denied ITC appeared first on TaxGuru .

articles.title: Loan Addition Sent Back to AO as Repayment Documents Required Verification
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that bank documents showing loan repayment must be verified before deciding a tax case under Section 68. The matter was sent back to the Assessing Officer (AO) for fresh examination. This decision impacts how tax authorities handle loan repayment evidence in income tax assessments.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal found that bank documents showing repayment of the loan were relevant for deciding the Section 68 issue. articles.summary: The Allahabad High Court set aside a GST detention order against a goods owner, ruling that proceedings should have been conducted under Section 129(1)(a) of the GST law. The court directed fresh action based on invoice valuation. The decision underscores the legal protections available to goods owners in detention disputes under India's tax framework.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the levy of Goods and Services Tax (GST) on online gaming, ruling that it is taxable as 'betting and gambling'. The court rejected the plea of Gameskraft Technologies Private Limited, stating that GST is a tax on supply, not profits. The ruling clarifies that tax liability applies to the gross value of services, not net outcomes after games.

SC Upholds State Power to Ban Betting on Skill Games
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that state governments have the authority to regulate or prohibit betting on skill-based games. The court held that Entry 34 of the Constitution, which deals with betting and gambling, applies to all forms of such activities. This decision empowers states to impose restrictions on skill games involving betting.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court has directed the removal of encroachments and illegal resorts in the Srivilliputhur-Megamalai Tiger Reserve. It also ordered the relocation of all government establishments within the Megamalai reserve region within six months. The Court's directives aim to protect the tiger reserve's ecological integrity and enforce environmental regulations. The order applies to both private encroachments and government facilities operating in the reserve.
articles.raw_summary: The Court also directed the relocation of all govt establishments in the Megamali reserve region within 6 months.

The court dismissed pleas arguing the EC lacked legal power, stating that SIR is traceable to the legal framework and meets the proportionality test. This ruling reinforces the EC's role in electoral processes.

articles.summary: The Supreme Court set aside a GST (Goods and Services Tax) order after finding that natural justice was violated. The court ruled that merely uploading a show-cause notice (SCN) on the GST portal after cancelling a taxpayer's registration was insufficient. This decision highlights procedural fairness requirements in tax administration and could impact future GST enforcement actions.

It held that after cancellation of registration, mere uploading of a notice on the GST portal was insufficient. articles.title: Assam passes Uniform Civil Code bill
articles.summary: The Assam state government has passed a Uniform Civil Code bill that prohibits polygamy and requires couples in live-in relationships to register with authorities. The legislation aims to standardize civil laws in the state and address social practices through legal enforcement. The bill was passed by the state legislature, marking a significant policy decision with immediate legal consequences.
articles.raw_summary: The legislation will ban polygamy and make it mandatory for live-in relationships to be registered.

articles.title: SC upholds special intensive revision of voter rolls, says exercise ‘advances free and fair polls’
articles.summary: The Supreme Court upheld the Election Commission's special intensive revision of voter rolls, stating it advances free and fair elections. The bench clarified that the Commission's inquiries during the process do not determine a person's citizenship. The ruling reinforces the legal framework governing electoral rolls and the Commission's authority.
articles.raw_summary: However, the bench said that the Election Commission’s inquiries during the process do not mean that it can decide on a person’s citizenship.

articles.title: Supreme Court Monthly Criminal Law Digest: April 2026
articles.summary: Anticipatory Bail - Routine filing and entertainment of anticipatory bail applications in private complaint cases is unnecessary, as mere issuance of summons/process does not entitle the police to arrest the accused. Sessions Courts and High Courts have no jurisdiction to direct an accused to surrender before the trial court while rejecting an anticipatory bail application in a complaint case. The Court may reject the application but cannot compel surrender. Om Prakash Chhawnika v. State of Jhar

articles.title: ITAT Rejects Blanket Bogus Purchase Disallowance as Sales & Stock Records Were Accepted
articles.summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) ruled that the tax department cannot disallow 20% of purchases as bogus if sales, stock records, and quantitative details are accepted. The tribunal stated that the mere failure to serve notices under Section 133(6) does not justify treating all purchases as invalid. This decision impacts tax assessment practices and reinforces procedural fairness in tax audits.
articles.raw_summary: The Tribunal held that disallowance of 20% of total purchases was unsustainable where the Assessing Officer had accepted sales, stock records, and quantitative details. Mere non-service of notices under Section 133(6) could not justify treating all purchases as bogus. articles.title: Permanently Vesting Secular Functions Of Mathadhipati In Govt Officer Denies Concept Of Mahantship; Violates Article 26: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that permanently vesting secular functions of a Mathadhipati (religious head) in a government officer violates the concept of Mahantship and breaches Article 26 of the Constitution. The Court set aside Andhra Pradesh government orders removing Arjun Dass as the Mathadhipati of the Sri Swamy Hathiramji Mutt in Tirupati. This decision underscores the separation of religious and administrative roles in governance.

The strong participation, especially among women, highlights ongoing discussions about electoral engagement and gender parity in local governance.

While polling remained largely peaceful, isolated incidents led to cancellations and postponements in a few wards, as counting for several local body posts got underway.

Both nations are capturing immediate opportunities.

articles.title: Around 80% polling in phase-II of Himachal panchayat elections; women surpass men in turnout
articles.summary: In the second phase of Himachal Pradesh's panchayat elections, voter turnout reached 79.79%, up from 76% in 2021. Women voters surpassed men with an 82.97% turnout compared to 76.69% for men. The higher participation, especially among women, highlights trends in electoral engagement that could influence future governance or policy decisions on inclusive voting practices.
articles.raw_summary: The percentages of female and male voters stood at 82.97% and 76.69%, respectively. The poll percentage, which was 76% in the second phase of Panchayat polls in 2021, increased to 79.79% in the second phase in 2026, officials said.

The resignations could lead to fresh byelections and renewed scrutiny over anti-defection laws. The shift in political dynamics may prompt discussions on electoral integrity and party discipline in the state.

articles.title: 'GST Is Tax On Supply, Not Profits' : Why Supreme Court Rejected Casino's Plea That Tax Liability Is On Net Outcome After Games
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that Goods and Services Tax (GST) on casinos applies to the total bet value, not the net outcome after games. The Court rejected a casino's plea that tax liability should be based on gross gaming receipts. This decision clarifies the tax framework for the gaming industry under India's GST regime.
articles.raw_summary: The Court held that the tax will be on the bet value, and not on the Gross Gaming Receipts.

articles.title: ED gets major manpower boost; action against economic offences set to become faster and more aggressive
articles.summary: The Centre has approved a major restructuring of the Enforcement Directorate (ED), significantly increasing posts across executive, legal, adjudication, and support roles. The decision is expected to make the ED's actions against economic offences faster and more aggressive.

articles.summary: OpenAI announced plans to strengthen its cybersecurity defenses and anti-misinformation efforts in preparation for global elections scheduled in 2026. articles.title: RBI books ₹1.69 lakh crore forex gains amid record dollar sales
articles.summary: The Reserve Bank of India earned ₹1.69 lakh crore from foreign exchange transactions in FY26, up 52% from the previous year, as it sold a record $53.13 billion from forex reserves to support the rupee amid a 9.5% depreciation. RBI’s total foreign income rose 27% to ₹3.28 lakh crore, helped by higher returns from foreign securities, while domestic income increased 26% to ₹1 lakh crore due to higher interest on rupee securities.

The decision has significant implications for the taxation of online gaming and fantasy sports in India.

articles.title: Hostile Witness Testimony Can Support Acquittal Too, Not Merely Conviction: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that testimony from a hostile witness can support an accused person's acquittal if it creates serious doubts about the prosecution's evidence. This clarification comes in a case where the witness turned hostile during trial. The ruling underscores that hostile witness testimony is not solely useful for convictions but can also aid in acquittals.
articles.raw_summary: Supreme Court clarifies that hostile witness testimony can be used to acquit an accused if it creates a serious doubt about the prosecution's evidence.

articles.title: Absence of Accused’s Name in Inquest Report Not Proof of Innocence: Supreme Court
articles.summary: The Supreme Court ruled that the absence of an accused person's name in an inquest report does not prove their innocence. The Court clarified that inquest reports only determine the apparent cause of death and cannot weaken a prosecution case on their own. This judgment reinforces the legal principle that such reports are not conclusive evidence in criminal proceedings.
articles.raw_summary: Inquest reports only ascertain the apparent cause of death; absence of the accused’s name cannot by itself weaken the prosecution, says the Supreme Court.

articles.title: Predator-prey relationships and habitat quality shape a tiger reserve
articles.summary: In 2009, Panna Tiger Reserve in Madhya Pradesh lost all its tigers to poaching. After reintroduction, a study found that prey availability and habitat quality are crucial for tiger population recovery. The findings underscore the need for balanced conservation strategies beyond just reintroducing carnivores. The research was published by Mongabay India.
articles.raw_summary: In 2009, Panna Tiger Reserve in Madhya Pradesh lost all its tigers to poaching. They were reintroduced a few years later and have since re-established populations. While carnivore reintroduction has been considered as an important strategy to restore ecological balance, a recent study highlights that prey abundance and habitat quality also play an essential role. [ ]

articles.title: Incoming western disturbance seen blocking monsoon path
articles.summary: A western disturbance in the atmosphere may block the monsoon's path over India, potentially delaying its onset. The monsoon is vital for agriculture, water supply, and the economy. The Hindu BusinessLine reports that atmospheric conditions over land are becoming resistant, adding to earlier concerns about oceanic support for the monsoon. This could impact farming and water availability if the monsoon is delayed or weakened.
articles.raw_summary: If it was the seas that seemed reluctant to support the onset so far, the atmosphere over land may also be turning resistant now

articles.title: ONGC production outlook improving on BP partnership, KG ramp-up: ICICI Securities

The article also notes that recent fuel price hikes have reduced losses for oil marketing companies. The analysis underscores the role of policy and market conditions in shaping India's energy sector performance.

articles.title: Govt open to views on capital gains tax cuts amid FPI outflows: Sitharaman

Finance Minister Nirmala Sitharaman stated the government is open to reviewing capital gains tax cuts amid these outflows. The record sell-offs highlight concerns over India's attractiveness to foreign investors and potential policy responses to stabilize markets.
articles.raw_summary: FPIs were net sellers of securities worth a record ₹1.8 trillion in FY26, the highest in 34 years and up from ₹1.3 trillion in FY25. The highest outflows were seen in March 2026 at around ₹1.18 trillion.

articles.title: India may have found a new export tailwind
articles.summary: India's weakening rupee is boosting export competitiveness. This adjustment helps Indian goods and services become more affordable globally. However, sustained export growth hinges on strong international demand and stable domestic inflation. The government highlights India's strong macroeconomic position amidst these developments. This situation presents a potential tailwind for Indian trade.

articles.title: India faces test of oil, inflation and monsoon risks despite economic resilience, FinMin report shows
articles.summary: India's economy shows cautious resilience. Domestic strengths support the outlook despite global challenges. Manufacturing and services are growing. Labour markets are stable. Foreign exchange reserves offer protection. However, rising crude prices, tighter finances, and a weak monsoon pose risks. Inflation needs careful watching. Navigating the coming year requires adaptability to maintain growth and control prices.

articles.title: Gold demand drops 70% after sharp duty hike in India
articles.summary: Demand fell to about 7.5 tonnes in the fortnight ended May 27 from around 25 tonnes a year earlier, according to industry estimates. The government increased the import duty on gold to 15% from 6% with effect from May 13.

articles.title: Tantrum of a problem child might cool India's economic engine
articles.summary: The IMD has retained its forecast for a below-normal monsoon in 2026, with El Nino expected to dominate the June-September season and rainfall likely at 90% of the long-term average. An HDFC report warns this could hurt farm output, raise food inflation and weaken rural consumption.

articles.title: Housing price index rises 4.2% in Q4 FY26: RBI
articles.summary: India's housing prices saw a significant rise. The All India House Price Index increased by 4.2 percent in the January-March quarter of 2025-26. This growth outpaced the previous year's expansion. Cities like Nagpur, Jaipur, Chandigarh, and Kanpur led this surge. Housing prices across Jaipur, Lucknow, and Pune also contributed to a 0.2 percent quarter-on-quarter increase.

articles.title: India's $360 billion infra investments slash logistics cost to 10-10.7% in FY26: CII-Knight Frank
articles.summary: India has significantly cut logistics costs through infrastructure investment. However, the nation requires more Multimodal Logistics Parks to achieve greater efficiency. These parks are crucial for shifting freight from roads to more cost-effective modes like rail. This strategic development will boost India's export competitiveness and manufacturing growth, creating an integrated freight economy for the future.

The discussions focused on expanding partnerships across trade, investment, innovation, and supply chains to drive shared prosperity between the two nations.

The Agriculture Ministry said better monsoon rains and higher minimum support prices (MSP) supported record crop production, with wheat output projected at an all-time high of 120.65 million tonnes.

The Agriculture Ministry said better monsoon rains and higher minimum support prices (MSP) supported record crop production, with wheat output projected at an all-time high of 120.65 million tonnes.

articles.title: China’s industrial profits surge at fastest in over two years
articles.summary: China's industrial profits rose by 24.7% in April, the fastest in over two years, fueled by demand for AI-related goods and higher oil prices stemming from the Iran war. However, weak demand continues to pressure downstream factories. The surge highlights China's economic recovery and its role in global supply chains, which could impact India's trade, energy imports, and technology sector policies.

articles.title: Right to trauma care of citizens integral part of right to life: SC
articles.summary: The Supreme Court of India ruled that the right to trauma care is an integral part of the right to life under Article 21 of the Constitution. The court emphasized that trauma care is critical in emergencies like accidents, where victims often experience shock and helplessness. This ruling underscores the state's obligation to ensure timely medical assistance. The decision has broad implications for healthcare policy and emergency services in the country.
articles.raw_summary: The top court said that when a person suffers an accident or any similar incident which requires urgent trauma care, they usually feel shock and disorientation, a sense of helplessness, where they have to hope that those around them will somehow help them get the care that they need.

articles.title: Huawei plans to build 1.4nm chips without the machine that powers TSMC, Samsung, and Intel

This development could impact global semiconductor supply chains and India's push for domestic chip manufacturing under initiatives like the Semiconductor Mission.

articles.title: Mint Explainer: Why India wants to go back to standard edible oil pack sizes
articles.summary: The government plans to bring back standard pack sizes for edible oils on complaints that similar-looking pouches with different quantities and prices were making value-for-money comparisons difficult for buyers. The move aims to improve transparency and ease shopping choices.

articles.title: ETtech Explainer: Why Google was fined Rs 30 lakh in Hindware trademark case
articles.summary: Google must pay Hindware 30 lakh rupees. The Delhi High Court ruled Google infringed on Hindware's trademark. Google is permanently stopped from using 'HINDWARE' as an advertising keyword. This decision impacts digital advertising and trademark law in India. Competitors used Google's platform to show their ads when users searched for Hindware products.
